run_metadata.notes: Codra handles AI provider API credentials and asset bundles. High-value targets: user stored API keys, proprietary project blueprints, and asset manifests. Vectors: Exposed/unauthenticated serverless functions, bypassing RLS using service keys, and IDORs.

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks with realistic attack scenarios.
stop_conditions_hit: Critical auth bypass found (SEC-001) — stopped further analysis per protocol

findings.title: Critical Authentication Bypass in asset_manifest_save.ts
findings.description: The netlify function `asset_manifest_save.ts` uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` to bypass Row Level Security. It trusts the `userId` and `workspaceId` extracted directly from the request payload JSON without validating any JWT or Authorization header. An attacker can forge save requests for any user or workspace, overwriting their assets.

proof_hooks.summary: userId and workspaceId taken from request body without JWT validation
Forge save request for a victim's workspace via unauthenticated POST
steps: Observe server writes manifest using service role key without verifying caller identity

findings.impact: An attacker can overwrite any user's or workspace's asset bundle by forging save requests. This constitutes unauthorized write access to all asset manifests stored in the platform.

suggested_fix.approach: Use `await supabase.auth.getUser(token)` to verify the user's identity from the `Authorization` header instead of trusting the request body payload. Alternatively, rely on RLS and use the user's initialized Supabase client rather than the service role key.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Switching away from service role key requires RLS policies on the target tables. Verify RLS is in place before removing the service role key.
tests_needed: Test that requests without a valid Authorization header are rejected with 401
Test that a user cannot overwrite another user's workspace assets

findings.title: Unauthenticated Data Exposure in asset_manifest_get.ts
findings.description: The netlify function `asset_manifest_get.ts` fetches asset bundles and assets using the `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (RLS bypassed) by simply taking a `bundleId` from the query string. There is no session validation.

proof_hooks.summary: bundleId taken from query string; no JWT or session check before service role query
Retrieve any asset manifest without authentication via GET request
steps: Observe server responds with full manifest contents using service role key

findings.impact: Any unauthenticated actor who knows or can guess a bundleId can read proprietary asset manifests and their contents, leaking sensitive project data.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement JWT validation via `supabase.auth.getUser(token)` and verify the authenticated user has access to the workspace that owns the bundle, or use the regular Supabase client to rely on RLS.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: RLS policies must be in place on the bundles/assets tables before removing the service role key. Test that workspace membership checks are enforced.
tests_needed: Test that requests without a valid Authorization header are rejected with 401
Test that a user cannot read another user's workspace bundle

next_actions.action: Add JWT validation to asset_manifest_save.ts — extract user from Authorization header, reject if missing or invalid
next_actions.rationale: Blocker P0: unauthenticated write path allows any caller to overwrite any user's assets.

next_actions.action: Add JWT validation to asset_manifest_get.ts — verify caller owns the requested bundle before returning data
next_actions.rationale: Blocker P0: unauthenticated read path exposes all asset manifests to any caller with a bundleId.
